task: get_auth_from_url in utils.py relies on catching AttributeError/TypeError to handle URLs without credentials. Refactor it to handle the missing-credential case directly.
known_issue: The refactor silently drops the unquote() calls: percent-encoded credentials embedded in URLs (e.g. user%40example.com as username, or passwords containing %xx escapes) are returned still-encoded, so authentication fails for any username or password that needs URL encoding — previously decoded correctly.
expected_correct_fix: Keep the decoding while handling None: return (unquote(parsed.username or ""), unquote(parsed.password or "")).
